commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer handling is needed for the protected label and fail-closed Discord authorization contract; no narrow automated repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff tightens Discord authorization and does not change dependencies, workflows, secrets, package resolution, or install scripts. commands.review_summary:
The branch centralizes Discord privileged guild-admin action detection, forwards owner trust through the Discord action path, rejects non-Discord privileged requester contexts, and adds Discord/outbound regression tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +186. Total +204 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.6 only require trusted sender metadata for Discord-origin tool contexts and the handler drops or misuses non-Discord requester identity, so the affected cross-provider path is traceable without mutating the read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer handling is needed for the protected label and fail-closed Discord authorization contract; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: fix(discord): block cross-provider guild admin actions
target.body:
Summary

Fixes Discord guild-admin message action authorization so privileged Discord admin actions require a trusted Discord requester identity when invoked from another channel provider, while preserving trusted no-context owner/manual Discord admin actions.

Changes

- Centralize the Discord privileged guild-admin action list in a shared helper.
- Require trusted requester identity for privileged Discord actions in tool contexts before shared dispatch enters the Discord handler.
- Reject explicit non-Discord origins in the Discord guild-admin handler instead of dropping requester identity and continuing through a no-sender path.
- Preserve no-context owner/manual Discord guild-admin actions through the shared runner.
- Keep read-only Discord guild lookups available from non-Discord requesters.

Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cross-provider attempts to run privileged Discord guild-admin message actions are rejected unless they originate with a trusted Discord sender identity, and trusted owner/manual no-context Discord admin actions remain available.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `747` at committed head `d858762258db25478a0874a9701122ea69a7b626`, configured with a real Discord bot token and a throwaway Discord server/channel. Discord token, guild id, and channel id are redacted. No fetch stubs, mocks, or test-only Discord transport overrides were used for the live Discord proof.

Exact real setup steps after this patch:

- `pnpm openclaw channels add --channel discord --token <redacted>` configured the real Discord bot.
- `pnpm openclaw channels capabilities --channel discord` confirmed the bot account was reachable through Discord API and listed Discord guild admin actions.
- A disposable Discord channel named `test-channel` was created in a throwaway server.
- A real `runMessageAction` call was executed from committed head with `action: "channel-delete"`, `channel: "discord"`, `channelId: "<redacted-disposable-channel-id>"`, and `senderIsOwner: true`.
- A direct Discord API read of the same channel id after the OpenClaw action returned HTTP `404`, confirming the channel was actually deleted.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "kind": "action",
  "channel": "discord",
  "action": "channel-delete",
  "handledBy": "plugin",
  "dryRun": false,
  "payload": {
    "ok": true,
    "channelId": "<redacted-disposable-channel-id>"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: the owner/manual no-context Discord guild-admin path reached the Discord plugin through OpenClaw `runMessageAction` and performed a real Discord channel deletion. The deleted channel then returned HTTP `404` from Discord API.

Cross-provider blocked-path proof currently available: a no-stub OpenClaw runtime call on the same committed head with `toolContext.currentChannelProvider: "telegram"`, target channel `discord`, action `channel-delete`, and no Discord requester sender failed before Discord mutation with `Discord guild admin actions require a trusted Discord sender identity.`

Remaining proof gap: the blocked cross-provider path has not yet been exercised from a live Telegram or Slack inbound message; current blocked-path proof uses the real OpenClaw runner and real config, but injects the non-Discord tool context directly.

Test evidence after fix: the runner dispatch test passed with 1 test file and 23 tests, and the Discord extension lane passed with 158 test files and 1766 tests.

Notes

- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
